label: Bilateral mirror layout — square render → flipTOP → side-by-side composite into portrait canvas
context: The 'two intelligences meeting / Three Versions meeting at the mirror' framing in TD-side composition. Render the SA cloud once at 720×720, then flip horizontally and place left+right halves of a 720×1280 portrait canvas. Invisible centerline seam; clouds are perfectly bilaterally symmetric.

operations.notes: Renders 720×720 source at half-size (360×360) shifted to left half of 720×1280 portrait canvas. tx=-0.25 fraction (=180px shift left). Vertical center.
Mirrored copy of render. Same source — symmetry guaranteed.
Same scale as xform_left, shifted to right half (tx=+0.25). Receives flipped source.

Add blend — non-overlapping halves so add behaves identically to over. Invisible seam at canvas center because both sides produce black at the centerline (extend='zero' fills outside source with transparent/black).
Final output buffer. 720×1280 portrait. Will become the input to the HDR post chain (move_004).

verified: two clouds visible at left/right halves of portrait canvas, perfect mirror symmetry
no visible seam at centerline (both sides black-zero at centerline)
open_decisions: Each eye is 360×360 in 720×1280 — leaves 280px black bars top+bottom (cinematic letterbox). Can re-scale in move_005 if eyes feel too small after HDR post lifts the visual weight.
Could alternatively render at 360×720 (taller-than-wide native cloud aspect) for larger eyes — would require camera/scene adjustment. Current path keeps cloud square + accepts letterbox.
next_move: move_004 — HDR cinematic post chain: hsv_mute (saturationmult=0.78) → level_post (crushed blacks inlow=0.06, roll highlights inhigh=0.95, contrast=1.18, gamma=0.92 cinematic S-curve) → bloom_post (output='inputplusbloom', threshold=0.4, intensity=1.1, maxbloomradius=0.22). Lifted from iris_2 final params.
